summary: Mechanical code-style cleanup labeled code-style: clang-format touched 36 files, mostly stage files under src/melee/gr plus one effect file and one menu file. Evidence shows include reordering, wrapping a long commented-out efSpecial call, wrapping a grPushOn prototype, and collapsing multi-line OSReport argument lists for repeated stage gobj allocation failure paths. No PR body, reviews, issue comments, validation notes, or decomp matching claims were present.

what_changed: PR title states the intent as running clang-format, and the PR carries a code-style label in raw PR metadata.
36 files changed with 48 additions and 78 deletions according to PR metadata; changed_files.jsonl lists one effect file, 34 stage-related files, and one menu file.
Visible diff hunks are mechanical formatting: include reordering, long-line wrapping, and reflow of OSReport argument lists.
Repeated OSReport calls of the form "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)" were reformatted in many gr* stage helper functions without visible expression or string changes.
No PR body, review comments, issue comments, or validation/matching report is available in the loaded evidence.

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR scope mechanical and style-only in visible evidence, which helps reviewers treat it as formatting rather than decomp logic work.
Applied consistent formatting across many similar stage gobj helper error-report paths, reducing local style variance without introducing semantic names or decomp tactics in the visible hunks.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For historical PR intake, treat broad clang-format-only PRs as style context, not evidence of decomp matching intent or validation.

Formatting changes may touch headers, include ordering, comments, and repeated report-call line breaks across many files; do not infer symbol semantics or source-authorship choices from such mechanical hunks alone.

follow_up_queries: PR 2311 clang-format OSReport couldn't get gobj

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, state, author, merge time, file list, and mechanical formatting intent.

Raw diff and diff excerpt are truncated, so detailed hunk content for grtyoshi.c tail, grtzelda.c, and mnruleplus.c is not fully visible in loaded evidence; their inclusion is supported by changed_files.jsonl.
No PR body, reviews, review comments, issue comments, or validation output was available; review feedback and match-status claims are therefore intentionally empty.
No evidence supports naming, assembly, objdiff, or decomp matching tactics beyond a broad formatting cleanup.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat clang-format-only PRs as style context, not decomp intent
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2311 is a merged code-style cleanup titled Run `clang-format`; evidence shows mechanical wrapping and include-order changes with no body, comments, reviews, or validation notes. Use it as formatting context only, not as proof of symbol semantics, matching tactics, or reviewer intent.

source_update_candidates.title: Optional note: formatting-only PRs should not be mined for decomp semantics
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that clang-format or code-style-only PRs can provide style evidence but should not be used as support for naming, matching tactics, validation, or authored-source intent unless comments or code changes explicitly show that.
evidence_refs: metadata counts showing no comments or reviews
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR is a clear example of broad mechanical formatting with weak evidence for decomp-specific lessons; curator may decide whether this belongs in standards or intake guidance.

rejection_notes: Do not record any match, objdiff, or build-validation lesson from this PR; no such evidence is present.
Do not infer naming conventions from address-style symbols such as fn_802190A0 or grT* functions in this PR; they were merely present in formatted lines.
Do not treat include reordering shown here as an accepted global include-order rule without checking the repository's formatter configuration or current standards.
